topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, a suffocating blanket against my skin. I’d been tasked with clearing out some old boxes, a chore that felt monumentally pointless. Then, tucked beneath a stack of brittle photo albums, I found them: every single assignment from high school, meticulously cataloged, folded neatly in acid-free paper. My hands, usually steady, trembled slightly as I lifted the collection. It wasn't just paperwork; it was a monument to a relentless, unseen effort. I sank onto an old trunk, the weight of the organized chaos pressing down on my chest. I just stared at the neat handwriting, a profound exhaustion settling deep into my bones.

[story 2]

I hadn't slept properly in three days. The relentless cycle of deadlines and self-imposed pressure had left me feeling hollowed out, like an empty vessel. While searching for a misplaced library book, I stumbled upon a locked wooden box in my mother’s study. Curiosity, a faint spark in the gloom, nudged me open. Inside, stacked spine-to-spine, were years of schoolwork. Each crumpled sheet, each neatly typed essay, spoke of an unwavering dedication I’d never witnessed firsthand. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips. It wasn't joy I felt, just a deep, profound sense of depletion, as if suddenly all the energy required to simply *exist* had been drained away.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the basement office hummed with an irritating monotony. I was supposed to be sorting old tax documents, but my focus kept drifting. Behind a filing cabinet, I found a large, sealed archival box. When I finally pried it open, the sight of hundreds of neatly organized school projects—math proofs, poetry drafts, science reports—hit me with unexpected force. I leaned against the cool metal of the cabinet, my shoulders slumping forward. My jaw felt heavy, and I couldn't summon the strength to lift the box again. It was overwhelming, this tangible evidence of constant, unyielding labor, and all I could manage was a quiet, almost inaudible shake of my head.
